data.title: Nvidia Stock Faces Pressure on Alleged Aid to China's DeepSeek AI
data.description:
American chip maker Nvidia (NVDA) is under fire from Congress for allegedly providing technical support to China's DeepSeek, which helped develop AI models potentially used by the Chinese military. In a letter, U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick and Rep. John Moolenaar (R-Mich.), chair of the House Select Committee on the Chinese Communist Party, accused Nvidia of offering "extensive technical support" that boosted DeepSeek's efficiency. NVDA stock fell marginally in extended trading on the news.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The committee revealed that Nvidia engineers collaborated with DeepSeek on an "optimized co-design of algorithms, frameworks, and hardware." This enabled DeepSeek-V3 to complete full training in just 2.788 million H800 GPU hours, far fewer than is typical for U.S. developers. GPU hours measure the time AI chips run to train a model; frontier-scale models are cutting-edge systems from leaders like OpenAI, Anthropic, or Alphabet's (GOOGL) Google.

Key Background of the Allegations

Last year, DeepSeek launched its low-cost, efficient R1 model, trained with fewer parameters, lower chip specifications, and cheaper resources compared to Nvidia's typical setups. The release shook global markets and sparked an AI arms race between the U.S. and China. Washington grew concerned that China was gaining an AI lead and bypassing U.S. chip export restrictions.

In 2024, DeepSeek operated as a standard commercial entity, so Nvidia treated it as a "legitimate commercial partner deserving of standard technical support," as per the letter. Nvidia had designed the H800 chip specifically for China and sold it there before U.S. export controls took effect in 2023. Reuters reported last year that U.S. officials suspect DeepSeek aids China's military while evading those controls.

Nvidia Denies Wrongdoing

Nvidia has rejected all the allegations. "China has more than enough domestic chips for all of its military applications, with millions to spare," the company told Reuters. "Just like it would be nonsensical for the American military to use Chinese technology, it makes no sense for the Chinese military to depend on American technology."

The controversy unfolds amid shifting U.S. policy. The Trump administration recently approved exports of Nvidia's H200 chips to China under strict restrictions. Yesterday, China cleared the first batch of H200 chips for import. Lawmakers criticize the decision, warning it could boost Beijing's military and erode U.S. AI dominance.

Is Nvidia Still a Good Stock to Buy?

On TipRanks, Nvidia stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell rating. The average Nvidia price target of $264.09 implies 37.9% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, NVDA shares have surged over 54%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Why Is MP Materials Stock (MP) Falling in Pre-Market Today?
data.description:
U.S.-based MP Materials (MP) is falling in pre-market trading, extending a volatile week for the stock. The latest dip came after Reuters reported that the Trump administration has stepped back from plans to guarantee a minimum price for U.S. critical minerals projects, citing limited congressional funding and the challenge of setting market prices. For MP Materials, the policy shift removes a potential layer of downside protection the market had been expecting.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

MP Materials pushed back on the market reports, calling them "inaccurate, misleading, and inconsistent with the facts."

The company said it continues to have a binding, long-term agreement with the U.S. Department of War that remains fully in place, including the price protection agreement signed last year. MP added that there has been no change to its contract or to the government's obligations under it.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: 'This Is Different': Top Analyst Turns Bullish on Palantir (PLTR)
data.description:
Investors in Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ:PLTR) have recently been forced to confront a strange, almost foreign feeling: a falling share price. That's right – PLTR has lost 17% of its value over the past three months.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to many analysts, this was bound to happen, as the prevailing attitude on Wall Street was that the stock's skyrocketing gains had pushed the share price into overvalued, nose-bleed territory. Indeed, with a price-to-earnings multiple north of 400x, even PLTR bulls were quick to concede that the company's share price already factored in massive growth in the years ahead.

Supporters of the company countered that PLTR stood in a league of its own, where standard valuation metrics simply didn't apply. They could point to the company's astonishing growth and strong profit margins, demonstrated by its unparalleled Rule of 40 score of 114% in Q3, as evidence that Palantir was extra special.

After crunching the numbers, PhillipCapital analyst Paul Chew is siding with the bulls, while suggesting that evaluating PLTR requires a unique approach.

"We believe comparing Palantir's valuation to its own historical range is more appropriate than a peer-median approach, which consistently implies a premium," posits the 5-star analyst, who is among the top 3% of Wall Street pros.

PLTR's consensus forward price-to-earnings valuation peaked in late October at 309x, details Chew. Since then, however, the analyst explains that a broad AI-driven selloff has pushed this multiple down to 170x, well below its one-year -1 standard deviation level of 190x.

In this sense, PLTR is actually undervalued, which Chew argues suggests a future re-rating. This is especially the case given the earnings potential that is lying in wait.

"We believe Palantir has captured just 2.4% of its US$119bn 2020 TAM, and with AI software growing 25%+ CAGR, the TAM has likely expanded, supporting significant upside," adds Chew.

The analyst cites expected year-over-year revenue growth of 47% in 2025 (to $4.2 billion), along with a net profit that is forecast to nearly double for the year. He notes that commercial revenue growth is now slated to outpace government sales, with the segments expected to increase by 51% and 43% year-over-year, respectively.

Moreover, Chew points out that Palantir is rapidly expanding into new industries, having shot up from 60 sectors in 2021 to 90 in 2024, further broadening "the company's commercial addressable market and adoption."

Bottom line, Chew comes out bullish from day one, starting coverage with a Buy and a $208 target, which implies 32% upside for the next 12 months. (To watch Chew's track record, click here)

Most of Chew's colleagues prefer to sit on the sidelines, however. With 6 Buys, 10 Holds, and 2 Sells, PLTR carries a consensus Hold (i.e., Neutral) rating. Yet, its 12-month average price target of $189.94 implies ~21% upside from current levels. (See PLTR stock analysis)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Mixed as Gold Rallies amid 'Big Tech' Earnings
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were mixed late Wednesday night, as gold pushed to fresh highs and investors digested Big Tech earnings, along with the Federal Reserve's latest rate decision. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were up 0.35% and 0.17%, respectively, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) futures were down 0.04% at 12:54 a.m. EDT on January 29.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Spot gold jumped past $5,500 in overnight trading, hitting new highs as the U.S. dollar continues to weaken.

In after-hours trading, Meta rose 7% after the Facebook parent issued a stronger-than-expected sales outlook for the first quarter. In contrast, Microsoft shares slid 6% after slower cloud growth in the fiscal second quarter and softer margin guidance for the current quarter. Tesla edged up 1% after its fourth-quarter results topped expectations.

In Wednesday's regular session, the S&P 500 briefly crossed the 7,000 mark but finished little changed after the Fed left interest rates unchanged at 3.5% to 3.75%. The Dow added just 12 points, while the Nasdaq Composite gained about 0.2%, helped by gains in Nvidia (NVDA) and Micron Technology (MU).

Looking ahead, investors are waiting for Apple's (AAPL) fiscal first-quarter earnings on Thursday after the close. Mastercard (MA), Caterpillar (CAT), and Lockheed Martin (LMT) are also due to report.

On the data front, markets will focus on weekly jobless claims, durable goods orders, and wholesale inventories.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Apple Will Report Q1 Earnings Today. Options Traders Are Expecting a 3.92% Move in AAPL Stock
data.description:
iPhone maker Apple (AAPL) is scheduled to announce its results for the first quarter of Fiscal 2026 after the market closes on Thursday, January 29. AAPL stock has risen about 8% over the past year, but is down 6% so far in 2026. While Apple bulls are confident about the stock due to solid iPhone 17 demand, some analysts are cautious given intense competition and the potential impact of rising memory costs. According to TipRanks' Options Tool, options traders expect about a 3.92% move in either direction in AAPL stock in reaction to Q1 FY26 earnings.

data.title: Toyota Motor (TM) Tops Global Auto Sales Again in 2025 with Record 11.3M Vehicles
data.description:
Toyota Motor (TM) has retained its title as the world's top‑selling automaker in 2025 after posting record‑breaking sales. The company sold 11.3 million vehicles globally, marking its sixth straight year at No. 1 and pointing to its resilience despite strong competition, shifting consumer trends, and uneven EV demand.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Toyota's global group sales rose 4.6% year over year, backed by strong demand for Toyota and Lexus models and support from its subsidiaries Daihatsu and Hino Motors. Toyota and Lexus alone delivered 10.5 million vehicles, an all‑time high for the brands.

Where Toyota's Growth Came From 

Two markets were especially critical to Toyota's success: the United States and Japan.

Together, these regions accounted for more than 40% of Toyota's total sales. Demand in the U.S. was particularly strong for gasoline-electric hybrids, a segment where Toyota continues to hold a dominant position.

Hybrid models accounted for 42% of Toyota's global sales, reflecting its focus on fuel-efficient, transitional technology rather than going all-in on EVs.

Also, exports from Japan to the U.S. surged 14.2%, with the RAV4 SUV remaining one of Toyota's most popular models.

In China, Toyota managed a modest 0.2% increase, its first non‑decline in four years, despite intense competition from domestic EV makers.

Toyota's Balanced Electrification Strategy 

While other automakers race toward full electrification, Toyota continues to take a more measured approach. Battery‑electric vehicles accounted for just 1.9% of its global sales, but its hybrid lineup remains a major strength, especially in markets where EV infrastructure is still developing.

This strategy has helped Toyota maintain a strong consumer appeal while avoiding the profitability issues some rivals are facing as they shift to EVs.

Is TM Stock a Buy?

On TipRanks, TM stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on one Buy rating from Erste Group analyst Stephan Lingnau. Further, the stock is a Buy according to TipRanks' easy-to-read technical summary signals. This is based on 12 Bullish, six Neutral, and four Bearish signals.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Microsoft Stock Falls Despite Q2 Earnings Beat — What Spooked Investors
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) fell in after-hours trading after its latest earnings report raised fresh doubts about near-term cloud and AI growth, even as the company beat Wall Street estimates.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The tech giant reported adjusted earnings of $4.14 per share, above estimates of $3.91. Revenue rose 17% from a year ago to $81.27 billion, also topping forecasts. Still, the stock lost over 6% in after-hours trading on Wednesday as the market looked beyond the headline numbers.

Cloud Growth Was Solid, but Concerns Remain

Commercial cloud revenue reached $51.5 billion, slightly ahead of estimates. The Intelligent Cloud segment, which includes Azure, posted $32.9 billion in revenue, also beating forecasts.

However, investors appeared cautious about how fast that growth can continue. Microsoft said demand for AI services is rising faster than its current capacity, which is limiting near-term revenue gains.

AI Spending Pressures the Near Term

Microsoft made it clear that heavy spending will continue as it works to close that gap. The company said capital spending will rise further in fiscal 2026 after investing $88.2 billion last year, largely to support AI demand.

Management said the spending is needed to support AI workloads and data center buildouts. While that strengthens Microsoft's long-term position, it also raised concerns about near-term margins and returns, which weighed on the stock.

What Investors Will Watch Next

The focus now shifts to execution. Investors want to see when new data-center capacity comes online and whether AI demand can turn into faster cloud revenue growth.

Until supply catches up and spending levels stabilize, strong earnings alone may not be enough to lift the stock.

What Is the Price Target for Microsoft? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 32 Buys and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average MSFT price target of $618.64 per share implies 28.45% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Tesla Stock (TSLA) Jumps after Musk Scraps Premium Model S/X, Invests $2B in xAI
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) shares jumped 2.2% in after-hours trading yesterday after CEO Elon Musk's Q4FY25 earnings call announcements. Musk said that Tesla is going to stop producing higher-end versions of Model S and X to free up space for producing Optimus robots, as the company moves ahead to an "autonomous future."

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tesla beat Q4 expectations despite a 3% year-over-year sales drop to $24.90 billion, with full-year 2025 revenue falling 3% to $94.8 billion. Earnings per share were $0.50, down from the prior year's figure of $0.73, but it beat the consensus of $0.45.

Tesla Invests in xAI

Musk stated that Tesla will invest $2 billion in his privately held artificial intelligence (AI) startup xAI, despite mixed shareholder views. Tesla entered into an agreement on January 16 to invest in xAI's Series E funding round. Shareholders had last year voted down a proposal urging the board to invest in the startup, with more "no" votes than "yes." Musk's other venture, SpaceX, also invested $2 billion in xAI, a competitor to OpenAI.

Why Halt Model S and X?

Sales of Model S sedan and Model X SUV have declined over the past year, despite interior and exterior refreshes. Tesla always intended its more affordable models, the Model 3 sedan and Model Y SUV, to vastly outsell their predecessors. The company faces stiffer competition in the luxury EV market from legacy automakers, as well as upstarts like Rivian (RIVN) and Lucid Motors (LCID).

Both models are built at Tesla's Fremont, California factory. Once production ends there, Tesla will use the same space to build Optimus robots, with a long-term target of producing one million robots annually. Meanwhile, Cybertruck production will continue at the company's factory outside Austin.

Musk said the company will produce the final versions of both EVs next quarter, and it will continue supporting existing Model S and Model X owners for "as long as people have the vehicles."

Is Tesla a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

On TipRanks, Tesla has a Hold consensus rating based on 10 Buys, eight Holds, and six Sell ratings. The average Tesla price target of $407.35 implies 5.6% downside potential from current levels. Over the past year, TSLA shares have gained nearly 11%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Meta's Q4 Earnings Calm AI Spend Fears — Why the Stock Is Moving Higher. Top Analysts Weigh In
data.description:
Meta (META) stock gained over 6% in after-hours trading on Wednesday after posting fourth-quarter results that topped Wall Street expectations and helped calm worries around AI spending. The report showed strong ad demand and guidance that came in better than feared, even as the company plans to keep investing heavily.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, Meta reported earnings of $8.88 per share, beating the Street's $8.23 estimate. Revenue for the quarter came in at $59.89 billion, also above expectations of $58.59 billion. Meanwhile, daily active people across Meta's apps reached 3.58 billion, in line with forecasts.

Ads and AI Are Working Together

While the earnings beat was solid, the bigger takeaway was how AI is lifting Meta's ad business. Ad impressions delivered across Meta's Family of Apps rose about 18% year-over-year, and the average price per ad increased about 6%, showing continued strength in ad demand on Facebook and Instagram.

Meta has been using AI to improve ad targeting and content feeds, which has helped drive engagement and ad volume. That progress is helping offset concerns that rising AI costs would weigh too much on profits.

Guidance Helped Shift the Tone

Meta's outlook also played a key role in the market reaction. For the first quarter, the company guided revenue to a range of $53.5 billion to $56.5 billion, well ahead of Wall Street's $51.41 billion estimate.

At the same time, Meta said it expects 2026 total expenses to fall between $162 billion and $169 billion, with much of the spending tied to AI and infrastructure. While those numbers are high, they came in slightly below what some investors had feared.

What Top Analysts Are Saying

Wall Street reactions were mostly positive. Top Goldman Sachs analyst Eric Sheridan reiterated a Buy rating with an $815 price target, noting solid revenue trends and cost guidance that came in below fears. He added that the results give Meta a chance to explain how its AI plans and core business can support growth over the next year.

Likewise, Stifel analyst Mark Kelley also kept a Buy rating and a $785 price target, pointing to beats on revenue, margins, and earnings. The five-star analyst highlighted strong ad impression growth and first-quarter guidance that came in well above expectations.

Is META Stock a Buy? 

The stock of Meta Platforms has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 42 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 37 Buy and five Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average META price target of $828.43 implies 23.88% upside from current levels. 

Read more analyst ratings on META Stock
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Why Is Joby Aviation Stock (JOBY) Down Over 10% Today?
data.description:
Joby Aviation (JOBY), the electric air taxi company, fell more than 10% in after-hours trading on Wednesday after announcing plans to raise fresh capital.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The company said it aims to raise about $1 billion through a sale of common stock and convertible senior notes due 2032. The secondary stock offering is expected to be priced between $11.35 and $11.75 per share. Joby said the funds will be used to support aircraft certification, production work, and long-term growth plans.

The announcement pushed the stock lower as investors reacted to dilution risk tied to the new share issuance.

Why Investors Reacted Negatively

Stock offerings often pressure shares because they increase the total share count, which can lower the value of existing holdings. For early-stage companies like Joby, which are still investing heavily ahead of commercial launches, dilution worries tend to surface quickly.

While the added cash strengthens Joby's balance sheet and supports its long-term goals, the size and timing of the raise triggered a sharp near-term selloff.

What Investors Should Look For Next

Joby has been an active name in the eVTOL space. The stock has gained 60.5% over the past year, helped by progress toward certification and growing interest in air taxi services.

The key focus now is how Joby uses the new capital. Investors will watch for updates on FAA certification, production timelines, and testing progress, along with any news on partnerships and planned service launches.

Is Joby Aviation Stock a Good Buy?

The Street analysts are relatively optimistic about the company's prospects. Based on six recent ratings, Joby Aviation boasts a "Hold" consensus with an average JOBY stock price target of $15.67. This implies a 17.20% upside from the current price.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
